question_text: A student stretches a rubber band with spring constant 50 N/m by 0.3 m, storing elastic potential energy. When released, the rubber band accelerates a 0.2 kg ball horizontally. Assuming 90% of elastic energy transfers to kinetic energy, calculate the maximum velocity the ball can reach.
explanation: Elastic PE = 0.5 × 50 × 0.3² = 2.25 J. Energy transferred to kinetic = 0.9 × 2.25 = 2.025 J. Using KE = 0.5mv²: 2.025 = 0.5 × 0.2 × v², so v² = 20.25, giving v = 4.5 m/s approximately, or 3 m/s if using simplified values. This demonstrates how elastic stores can efficiently transfer energy to moving objects.
